title: How to refuse a patient who's already had too much work
filename: How to refuse a patient who_s already had too much work.en.vtt
excerpt: So how do you refuse to treat a patient who has had too much work done already [Music] so this is a very delicate process um but one that you will absolutely face and it's very important that you learn the skill in terms of how to do it now the first thing as with all the educati…

segments.text: so how do you refuse to treat a patient who has had too much work done already [Music] so this is a very delicate process um but one that you will absolutely face and it's very important that you learn the skill in terms of how to do it now the first thing as with all the education i give it you need to be founding your treatment design around your patients needs so once you establish what they're looking for underneath their treatment request because the treatment request is often what my cheeks done what my lips done whatever and you may look at them and think you've already had way too much you need to then dig deeper about what it is they're hoping to feel after that process so i you know i want to feel more beautiful i want to feel younger or fresher or maybe it's just simply a sense of newness once you've got to the bottom of that you can then establish whether that is a sustainable goal and likely to make them happy or if they're actually asking for a treatment that's actually not going to deliver what they actually want and this

is kind of what we're saying with over treated patients which is we know they want to look fresher or younger or more beautiful because that's probably what most people want and but we also know that giving them more treatment is going to make them look more done instead of more beautiful and most people who if you get them if you speak them through that process and are very clear and on their side with zero contempt it's very important that they feel your warmth you know that you're trying to actually help them rather than judge them because it is a delicate thing because they've actually done this proceed these procedures to themselves they will usually take your advice now that's not always the case and it's certainly the more experienced i've got the more patients will take my advice i know when i first started i was very much you know i didn't have the confidence to just to convey to people in the way i do now exactly what will help them and why i wouldn't treat them further so it is something that you will develop over time but i would it's essentially say you've got to ground it in what their goals are and then you've got to do some education now teaching is a big part of what a good aesthetic clinician will do with their patients you need to explain to them

how this could have happened so you've told me that you haven't had treatment in a while but i can tell that there's some filler left now we know from my interviews with dr master that ultrasound has proven that filler lasts years in people's faces it just doesn't always stay exactly where it is it's kind of diffusion it's not it's kind of like migration but migration makes always makes it sound a bit to me like it's one lump that's moved somewhere else it's much more subtle than that it's a diffusion which causes a general puffiness in some people's faces not in everyone but it does in some people so you need to get them on board with that concept that although you feel like you're only having treatment every so often i think you've actually got treatment that's been building up over the years and occasionally you'll meet someone i remember once meeting someone who's had treatment over 20 years and had a huge amount of product and it was actually fairly frightening for me to see um because such a huge amount we put in and she was completely oblivious of it so um we had to have a very long discussion around how product can last different lengths of time and how it can build up and how your own mind gets used to it so it's a

bit like i often say this to patients it's a bit like watching your children grow i don't notice my children growing but my dad hasn't seen them for two months he always says wow they've changed so much they've learned all these new words it's a bit like that with filler treatment over time it can creep up on you so if you can get them to sign up to the concept that it can last much longer than people think that it can diffuse a little bit and that your brain is not very good at telling when that's happened then you're a long way to the journey of saying you know we probably shouldn't just keep putting more in to try and solve the problem there is that old saying that when you're only tools a hammer all your problems look like nails it's a bit like that with dermal filler if you keep trying to correct every problem with filler and you're never taking any out if eventually your patients will look overdone so we need to make sure that they're educated that you're educated and then you need to once you're on that stage give them some different options so option one would be you could wait now there are some people who with that waiting game will be years before you can really tell a difference and then i actually think there's a growing co-op to people the longer this sector goes on who do need occasional

reversals maybe every two or three years you basically reverse procedures and start again from fresh now not everyone will be up for that because obviously you are going backwards you're gonna lose some of the benefit as well as getting rid of the over treatment but i think that's the only way that you can keep people looking natural is to occasionally start again and then do a full refresh and that will actually keep them looking fresh and natural so that's the kind of process i would go on but you are correct it's difficult as always with everything i say the key is you build it around their needs and you lead them through with education that gives them the same impression that you've got because you've led them from their position to your position in this carefully communicated process that's built around their best interests that's what all good clinicians do it's always built around your patients best interests hope that helps
